@@ -2,1 +2,1 @@ - * Copyright (c) 1994, 2025, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved. + * Copyright (c) 1994, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved. @@ -187,0 +187,1 @@ + *
  • {@code Double.equivalent(a, b)} @@ -1529,0 +1530,22 @@ + /** + * {@return {@code true} if the arguments have {@linkplain + * ##repEquivalence representation equivalence} and {@code false} + * otherwise} + * + * Special cases: + *
      + *
    • {@code -0.0} and {@code +0.0} are not equivalent + * (unlike how {@code -0.0} and {@code +0.0} compare under the + * {@code ==} operator) + *
    • two NaN values are equivalent to each other, but not + * equivalent to any non-NaN value + *
    + * + * @param d1 the first {@code double} to compare + * @param d2 the second {@code double} to compare + * @since 27 + */ + public static boolean equivalent(double d1, double d2) { + return Double.doubleToLongBits(d1) == Double.doubleToLongBits(d2); + } +

@@ -2,1 +2,1 @@ - * Copyright (c) 1994, 2025, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved. + * Copyright (c) 1994, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved. @@ -1317,0 +1317,22 @@ + /** + * {@return {@code true} if the arguments have {@linkplain + * ##repEquivalence representation equivalence} and {@code false} + * otherwise} + * + * Special cases: + *
    + *
  • {@code -0.0f} and {@code +0.0f} are not equivalent + * (unlike how {@code -0.0f} and {@code +0.0f} compare under the + * {@code ==} operator) + *
  • two NaN values are equivalent to each other, but not + * equivalent to any non-NaN value + *
+ * + * @param f1 the first {@code float} to compare + * @param f2 the second {@code float} to compare + * @since 27 + */ + public static boolean equivalent(float f1, float f2) { + return Float.floatToIntBits(f1) == Float.floatToIntBits(f2); + } +

@@ -2,1 +2,1 @@ - * Copyright (c) 2023, 2025, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved. + * Copyright (c) 2023, 2026, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved. @@ -1058,0 +1058,21 @@ + /** + * {@return {@code true} if the arguments have {@linkplain + * Double##repEquivalence representation equivalence} and {@code false} + * otherwise} + * + * Special cases: + *
    + *
  • negative zero and positive zero are not equivalent + *
  • two NaN values are equivalent to each other, but not + * equivalent to any non-NaN value + *
+ * + * @param f1 the first {@code Float16} to compare + * @param f2 the second {@code Float16} to compare + * @since 27 + */ + public static boolean equivalent(Float16 f1, Float16 f2) { + return Float.equivalent(f1.floatValue(), f2.floatValue()); + } + +
